channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: McCullum to leave as England Test coach
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Brendon McCullum will leave his role as England's Test head coach but continue to lead the white-ball teams.

item.title: Neville's goosebumps over Bellingham: I have never seen anything like it
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Gary Neville says that Jude Bellingham is England's superstar now and that he has never seen anything like it – claiming his performance gave him goosebumps.

item.title: Africa’s Gaming Industry Faces Balancing Act Between Regulation, Investment
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: As Africa’s gaming industry continues to expand, operators, regulators, and investors are grappling with the challenge of sustaining growth while strengthening regulatory oversight, consumer protection, and responsible gaming practices. These issues are expected to dominate discussions at the

item.title: 3% NIMASA levy should fund trucks, barges for indigenous operators
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Efe Onodjae The Federal Government has been urged to direct the Nigerian Maritime Administration and Safety Agency (NIMASA) to utilise the 3 per cent freight levy collected from international inbound and outbound cargo to provide revolving loans for indigenous maritime transport operators and lic

item.title: Umahi To Obi: I Have Broader Public Service Record, Insists On Performance Debate
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, has insisted that his challenge to former Anambra State Governor and presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Mr. Peter Obi, was intended to compare their records in public office rather than their political status. Umahi’s po

item.title: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Donald Trump paid tribute to the 71-year-old South Carolina senator, calling him a "true American patriot" who will be "greatly missed".

item.title: Taaooma Reveals How She Stays Creative and How Motherhood Changed Her Life
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian content creator Maryam Apaokagi, popularly known as Taaooma, has shared the secret behind the originality of her comedy skits, saying she draws inspiration from everyday life while being intentional about the content she consumes. Speaking on the sidelines of the United States Mission in Ni

item.title: LG poll: APC wins 18 chairmanship, 192 councillorship seats in Edo
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The chairman said the election was successfully conducted across the 18 local government areas and 192 wards in the state. The post LG poll: APC wins 18 chairmanship, 192 councillorship seats in Edo appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: IPPG Urges Bold Reforms To Unlock Nigeria’s Full Energy Potential
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Independent Petroleum Producers Group (IPPG) has called for bold, coordinated reforms to reposition Nigeria’s oil and gas industry from a resource-based economy to a globally competitive engine of industrial growth and national prosperity. Speaking at the opening ceremony of the 25th NOG E

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Some members of the community who spoke to the Nigerian Tribune on Sunday said that the assailants stormed the community in The post Suspected armed herders kill 16 in renewed Benue attack appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Governing Council of University of Abuja now known as Yakubu Gowon University has approved the promotion of 34 academic staff to professorial ranks and appointed substantive Registrar and University Librarian. 17 academics were promoted to the rank of Professor, while another 17 were elevated to

item.title: Namdas Hails Shettima, Says Tinubu’s Renomination Of VP Signals Stability
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The newly appointed Director General of the Border Communities Development Agency (BCDA), Hon. Abdulrazak Namdas, Ph.D has commended President Bola Tinubu’s decision to renominate Senator Kashim Shettima as the Vice Presidential candidate of the All Progressives Congress (APC) for the 2027 President

item.title: NDLEA busts South African woman using 3-year-old son as cover to smuggle heroin into Nigeria
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the agency, the suspect told investigators she travelled from Cambodia through Doha to Abuja. Preliminary intelligence, however, indicates that she is part of a transnational drug trafficking syndicate allegedly operated alongside her husband or partner, Jan Coenraad De Jager, who is ba

item.title: Vector rejects “weaker vessel” mentality over sibling traumas
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Vector revealed that while growing up, he was bullied by his sisters to the point of being traumatised. He noted that his childhood experience shaped how he sees women in regards to strength

item.title: World Cup: He makes wrong decision – Zlatan Ibrahimovic attacks Madueke, hails Bellingham
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Sweden striker, Zlatan Ibrahimovic, has hit out at Noni Madueke while also hailing Jude Bellingham after England’s World Cup quarter-final 2-1 win against Norway on Saturday. Ibrahimovic said Madueke made the wrong decision during the encounter against Norway, as he also lauded Bel

item.title: Blue Prince became a bonding — and learning — experience for my family
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: I've always been the gamer in the family. When my son was born nearly 11 years ago, gaming was one of the things I looked forward to sharing with him. Pulling up a chair next to me, he would watch as I played Sea of Thieves with friends, often wearing the too-big-for-his-head headset to talk [&#8230

item.title: The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: As earnings season kicks off, AI investment remains front and center.

item.title: Pastors must rebuke bad governments, not benefit from it – Odumeje criticises Adeboye

item.description: Popular Onitsha-based cleric and founder of the Mountain of Holy Ghost Intervention and Deliverance Ministry, Chukwuemeka “Odumeje” Ohanaemere, has said religious leaders have a biblical obligation to challenge poor governance rather than align themselves with those in power. His remarks

The post SERAP Drags INEC To Court Over Alleged Di

item.title: Governor Alia Condemns Deadly Benue Attack, Orders Security Agencies To Hunt Killers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Governor Hyacinth Alia, has condemned the deadly attacks on Akpachi Village and the Otukpo-Nobi community in Otukpo Local Government Area of Benue State, directing security agencies to track down and bring the perpetrators to justice. In a statement issued by his Chief Press Secretary, Tersoo Kula,

item.title: Nine family members killed in fresh Plateau attack
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Gunmen killed nine members of one family in Plateau State's Riyom LGA, while a village head was critically injured in the late-night attack. The post Nine family members killed in fresh Plateau attack appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Kingsley Omonobi Troops of the Nigerian Army’s 65 Battalion have intercepted a truck conveying a large consignment of illicit drugs along the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Road following credible intelligence. Acting Deputy Director, Army Public Relations, Lieutenant Colonel Musa Yahaya, disclosed this i

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Governor Hyacinth Alia has condemned the deadly attacks on Akpachi Village and the Otukpo-Nobi community in Otukpo Local Government Area of Benue State, describing the killings as a brutal assault on innocent lives and a threat to the state’s peace. The governor’s reaction followed the e

item.title: Gov Lawal Distributes 72,000 Free Bags Of Fertiliser To Zamfara Farmers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Zamfara State governor, Dauda Lawal, has distributed more than 120 trucks of fertiliser and essential farm inputs to farmers in the state. The Zamfara 2026 wet-season fertiliser programme was launched on Friday at the Trade Fair Complex in Gusau, the state capital. A statement by the governor’s spok

item.title: ‘Are You the Law?’ Peter Obi blasts Keyamo, alleges political targeting

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Luminous Jannamike ABUJA — The Bishop of the Anglican Diocese on the Niger, the Rt. Rev. Dr. Owen Nwokolo, has blamed Nigeria’s persistent leadership crisis on entrenched corruption rather than the government of the day, declaring ahead of the 2027 general elections that he expects ‘n

item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: When Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew walked into Phase III Section of the Federal Secretariat in Abuja with a forged letter and emerged with an office, signage, and a semblance of government legitimacy, he did not act alone. That is the uncomfortable truth of the “Presidential Foreign Intervention Pr

item.title: Embrace Gardening For Food Security, Bauchi First Lady Urges Women
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Wife of the Bauchi State Governor, Hajiya Aisha Bala Mohammed, has urged women and households to embrace home gardening as a sustainable means of improving family nutrition, reducing food expenses and enhancing food security. Speaking with journalists in Bauchi after she inspected her farms, the Fir

item.title: Replicate Oyo Rescue Operation In Kaduna, Borno, Kwara, ACF, NEF Tell FG
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Following the successful rescue of 44 pupils and teachers abducted in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State, the Northern groups, including the Arewa Consultative Forum (ACF) and Northern Elders Forum (NEF) have called on the President Bola Tinubu-led government to replicate the rescue operation

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Ugandan Army Chief Demands Jay-Z ‘Surrender’ Beyoncé

item.description: Muhoozi Kainerugaba, the Ugandan Chief of Defence Forces and son of President Yoweri Museveni, has sparked fresh online controversy after reiterating his public desire to marry American music star Beyoncé Knowles-Carter.   The military leader took to his verified X page to declare that he would

item.title: Insecurity: Borno youths protest, demand stronger action against terrorism
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Youths and students in Borno State on Sunday staged a protest in Maiduguri, calling for stronger action against terrorism and other forms of insecurity while urging Nigerians to unite in the fight against criminality. The protest, tagged UniteAgainstTerror, was organised by youth and

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The wife of Bauchi State governor, Hajiya Aisha Bala Mohammed yesterday paid a condolence visit to the family of former deputy governor of the state, Senator Baba Tela, following the tragic death of his two children, Mustapha and Ummi, in a road accident recently. Speaking during the visit, Hajiya A

item.description: Kuni Tyessi in Abuja The Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN) has hailed the rescue of pupils, teachers and other victims abducted from schools in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz after its naval forces fired warning shots at a vessel attempting to pass through the strategic waterway via what it described as an unauthorised route. The announcement was made early Sunday in a

item.description: A conservative Iranian newspaper has published a list naming United States President Donald Trump and several European leaders as potential revenge targets following the killing of Iran’s late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. item.description: Investors are rotating beyond AI winners in emerging markets, where just three technology stocks worth $4.4 trillion drive an outsized share of returns.

item.description: Young Ghanaian comedian, Abdul Latif Musah, popularly known as MUSAH, is steadily carving a niche for himself in Ghana’s digital entertainment space through his hilarious and relatable social media skits.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Kogi State Internal Revenue Service (KGIRS) has declared a total clampdown on illegal tax and revenue collection on highways across the state, warning that offenders will be arrested and prosecuted under the provisions of Nigeria’s new tax law. Ibezim, who represented Governor Chukwuma Soludo at the National Women Summit held in Abuja with the

item.title: Alvarez: Argentina Will Do Everything To Help Messi Win World Cup 2026

item.description: Atletico Madrid striker Julián Álvarez has declared that the Argentina squad will do everything to help Lionel Messi win another World Cup. Álvarez made the emotional comments following Argentina’s dramatic 3-1 quarter-final victory over Switzerland, in which he scored the decisive extra-time

item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Senegal have sacked manager Pape Thiaw in the aftermath of the country’s exit at the last-32 stage of the 2026 World Cup. The Teranga Lions, who had been managed by Thiaw since December 2024, were beaten by France and Norway in their opening two group games. They thrashed Iraq 5-0 to qualify f

item.title: Towards Building New Peace Architecture For Plateau
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: By Rabilu Haruna Peace is one of the few public policies that can not be legislated into existence. It can not be imposed by executive orders, negotiated solely in conference halls, or sustained through military deployments. Lasting peace grows from trust, and trust grows when people believe that th

item.description: Storm the size of France expected to bring days of heavy rains and strong winds to eastern and northern provinces.

item.title: How Founder Control Is Reshaping Public Markets
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: SpaceX’s record-setting IPO gave public investors access to one of the world’s most valuable companies, but not equal voting rights. item.title: The fight against AI data centers is just beginning
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the data center buildout, follow Emma Roth. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes on Sunday at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here. How it started Years before the AI boom threate

item.title: World Cup Bets on Prediction Markets May Get Tax Edge Over Gambling
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Americans using prediction markets to bet on the World Cup may face a lighter tax burden than peers wagering through sportsbooks thanks to tax breaks aimed at investments.

item.title: The CLAP BACK Post
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: Some people are too comfortable throwing comments that they can’t defend.They stir drama, twist narratives, and expect everyone to play along.That's not happening here....Clapback or dont complain!!

item.title: US senator and Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies aged 71
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Prominent US Senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of President Donald Trump, died aged 71 following a "brief and sudden illness," his office said on Sunday.

item.title: NDLEA arrests South African woman for heroin trafficking
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: .. says she used son as cover By Kingsley Omonobi The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has arrested a 38-year-old South African woman, Ms. Will Jessica Ann, at the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport (NAIA), Abuja, for allegedly attempting to smuggle 5.75 kilogrammes of heroin into Nige

item.title: NDLEA arrests South African woman with 5.75kg of heroin at Abuja airport
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency has arrested a 38-year-old South African woman attempting to smuggle 5.75 kilograms of heroin into Nigeria at the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport in Abuja. The post NDLEA arrests South African woman with 5.75kg of heroin at Abuja airport appeared first o

item.title: Gov Alia Condemns Fresh Killings in Otukpo Communities
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Benue State Governor Hyacinth Alia has condemned the latest attacks on communities in Otukpo Local Government Area by armed men, describing the killings as a barbaric and inhuman assault on the sanctity of life and the collective soul of the people of the state. The governor’s reaction followe

item.title: Woman uses 3-year-old son as cover to smuggle heroin into Abuja — NDLEA
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: NDLEA arrested a South African woman over an alleged heroin smuggling attempt and made multiple drug seizures in nationwide operations. The post Woman uses 3-year-old son as cover to smuggle heroin into Abuja — NDLEA appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Borno residents, youths rally behind security agencies against Boko Haram, kidnapping
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Hundreds of residents, students, youth groups and civil society organisations in Borno State on Sunday staged a peaceful solidarity rally in Maiduguri, declaring support for security agencies in the fight against Boko Haram, ISWAP, banditry and kidnapping, while calling on the Federal Government to

item.title: Peller, Jarvis Announce August 1 Wedding Date
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: TikTok stars Habeeb Hamzat, popularly known as Peller, and Amadou Elizabeth Aminata, better known as Jarvis, have announced August 1, 2026, as the date for their wedding. The couple made the announcement through a cinematic video shared across their social media platforms. The video concluded with t

item.title: Hormuz Reopening Faces Costly Hurdles
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Senior Fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations Clara Gillispie says Gulf energy flows face an uncertain recovery as producers work to clear ships, bring in tankers, restart halted output and repair damage to refineries, LNG facilities and ports. She says the Strait of Hormuz remains unstable, wit

item.title: Lagos court jails 67-year-old lawyer for environmental offences
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Olasunkanmi Akoni A Lagos State Special Offences (Mobile) Court has sentenced a 67-year-old lawyer, Taiwo Abidemi, to seven months’ imprisonment for violating the state’s environmental laws. Abidemi was convicted after pleading guilty to a three-count charge bordering on breach of pea

item.title: World Cup 2026 : England to face Argentina in tense semi-final
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Switzerland meanwhile is eliminated after losing to Argentina 3-1. Mac Allister gave the reigning champions the lead only ten minutes into the game, heading the ball past Gregor Kobel off a Leo Messi cross. Switzerland gained in momentum, culminating in an equalizer from Dan Ndoye. But that goal was

item.title: Yt-Dlp Sequence Diagrams
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://app.ilograph.com/demo.ilograph.yt-dlp/Download%2520a%2520YouTube%2520Video Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48880384 Points: 33 # Comments: 1
